title: Split generate-apply into compute and writer stages
description: Concrete refactor of _generate_apply_stream into a producer/consumer split: a compute stage (parallel via pool, reads ANLZ + generates cues) and a writer stage (single thread, commits to DjmdCue). Implements the architecture promised by TASK-002.
acceptanceCriteria: _generate_apply_stream factored into _compute_stage(content_ids, db, prefs, queue, cancel_event) and _writer_stage(queue, db, send_event, cancel_event).
Compute stage submits one pool.future per content_id; pushes results into the queue as as_completed.
Writer stage drains the queue, commits per-track, emits SSE.
Existing per-track commit semantics preserved (one failed track does not abort the batch).
Module structure makes it possible to reuse the same producer/consumer scaffolding in TASK-005, TASK-006.

steps.details: Pure-read function: takes (content_ids, db_reader, prefs, queue, cancel_event). Submits pool.submit(_compute_cues_for_track, ...) for each id. Iterates as_completed, pushes result into queue. On cancel_event.is_set(): drain in-flight, push sentinel None, return.

Threading.Thread target. Loops queue.get(); on None sentinel, break. For each result: open transaction, write cues, commit, emit SSE event with monotonic 'processed' counter. On per-track exception: rollback that single track, increment 'errors', emit error event.
The async SSE handler spawns the writer thread, calls _compute_stage in the request thread (or another worker), and yields events from a thread-safe send_event queue.
tests/test_serve_routes.py: existing tests work end-to-end; add focused unit tests for _compute_stage and _writer_stage in isolation (mock queue + cancel_event).

technicalNotes: If both stages share the same Rekordbox6Database instance, that instance must be thread-safe (TASK-008). Otherwise compute uses thread-local DB via thread_local_db().
The writer thread is the single owner of db.session for writes. The request thread must not call db.commit during the stream.
